channel.title: Comments for page "Population Control(led)"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Population Control(led)"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally think that would be a bit unwieldy to flesh out here. Perhaps another tale can expand on that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Obviously not everything is sterilized, just enough to keep the immediate problem of gross overpopulation at bay. In further tales there are references to medicinal maggots, and the fact that you can still eat things like steak and drink milk means that we know we can kill animals with the faith that they’ll come back.

Of course, you are right in pointing out that I only really talk about sterilizing insect populations, and the fact that I don’t go further into what actually gets done is an oversight on my end.

If I could find a way to smoothly amend this, I would. And maybe I will. I do appreciate you reading it though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, so, I need to clarify that the whole EoD hub is a pretty good read. But this particular article, especially its ending is just… so wrong. And I don't mean anything about how it's written. It's just this "we've made animals sterile, now everything's fine, yay!" that bugs me out. Seriously, ecosystems do not work this way.

Basically if you stop some species from reproducing, everything above them on the food chain will just eat out it's food supply and then starve to death, thats it. And if we are speaking insects and other vermin, whitch are going to be the main overpopulation threat in EoD scenario, than we have almost entire food chains above them. Which means that a global extinction event will hit the world in just a couple of years.

And even that is not all. Every flowering plant and anything else that evolved into symbiosis with some creepy-crawlies is also gone. And also most other plant life in a matter of decades, because, guess what, they happen to depend heavily on substances fron dead organics and forrowing of soil by little creatures, that the world have suddenly run out of.

So, in summary, such a descision would very, and I mean very, fast turn the world into a desolate wasteland almost devoid of life. That by itself, imho, would be an interesting topic to expand onto. But we instead get a family guy - style "glad that everything is back to normal" and that's it? Sorry but this is a solid downvote to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you read the rest of the Omega-K series, you'll find out why that's not gonna work ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, have these people never heard of an incinerator?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm still rather leery of WWS as a concept, so a tale focused entirely on their perspective was a hard sell for me. I appreciate the tone you tried to create but the combination of wilson being wilson and a large amount of exposition compared to payoff, and I just didn't really enjoy this tbh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, I feel super bad for Wilson's Wildlife Solutions here. It's so hard to imagine exactly how overwhelming this is for them, even with this tale. Excellently done conveying the seriousness of the situation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thus far I’ve loved this entire series so I’m gonna head back and +1 everything once I’m done reading it though.

Only concern of mine is regarding the overpopulation of insects. If I’m remembering correctly it’s stated that this doomsday anomaly does not prevent aging or offer a healing factor. Most bugs naturally have a very short lifespan before they die. In this scenario they don’t “die” but I would assume for the most part a lot of their functions deteriorate from old age like normal? Which would prevent the whole overpopulation issue. Or I could just know nothing about bugs lol.
It was just a thought of mine. It doesn’t really take away too much from the entire scenerio if it is an issue. I’m still gonna +1 everything because this whole thing is so grime and awesome to read.

oh god the cockroaches

The whole “but the world keeps spinning” concept but now we have to suffer through it as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 for a lion with its stomach exploded and full of maggots but still alive.
That's a mental image I'll be stuck with for a while..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was the story that I made with you in mind, I'm glad that you approve!

Of all the people I wanted to impress, I just wanted to be sure that you were okay with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
:D

I especially like that my boy Tim Wilson essentially thanks himself at the end there, thanking whoever is up there looking after him. ‘Cause alt. timeline him is the person looking after him. That’d cute. And agreed with the comments above; I like the hopeful turn. When things can’t die, make them not exist in the first place. That’s clever. Real dang clever. Good job!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm liking how this doomsday is equal parts dark and hopeful.

Me too. But I'm assuming something will go horrendously wrong though and humans will make themselves never have existed or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When Wilson's was first introduced, I wasn't too sure what to think about them, but I feel like, by now, I've come around to where I'm glad the Foundation is working side-by-side with a GoI for a very specific, if common, range of situations.

we did our best to make sure they were at least taken care of as best as we could.

Got a little redundancy there.

If nothing else, I'm liking how this doomsday is equal parts dark and hopeful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my God, this well done. I hadn't even taken into consideration what would happen if insect populations suddenly stoped dying when I read about your teams end of death scenario.

A definitive up-vote from me for taking such an interesting spin on an interesting situation, +1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
My second contribution to the Doomsday Contest!

Thanks to my team, of course, and also to Stallmantic for some crit!

(My team being Captain Kirby, Veiedhimaedhr & Croquembouche)
